news.title: OpenAI tripled revenue to $5.7 billion in Q1
news.description: In the first quarter of 2026, OpenAI pulled in $5.7 billion in revenue and burned through about $3.7 million . Stock-based compensation alone ate up over $2.3 billion . With $73 billion in reserves, the AI giant doesn't need fresh capital right now . Anthropic could change that fast, but a price war with Anthropic is a big threat to OpenAI .

news.title: ChatGPT keeps creeping toward becoming your AI personal
news.description: OpenAI is upgrading ChatGPT's scheduling feature . A new "Scheduled" page in the sidebar puts all active tasks in one place, letting users view, pause, edit, or delete them . Research tasks search the web and connected apps, sending alerts only when something actually changes . The previous "Pulse" feature is being retired, and the previous 'Pulse' feature is also being retired .

news.title: Data2Story turns a CSV file into a verified
news.description: "Data Journalist Agent" turns a CSV file into a verified interactive article with graphics, web research, and verifiable source links for 93 percent of all statements . In a reader study, 74 percent preferred the agent's output over the human original . But against elaborately crafted long-form reports, the agent managed a tie with 74 percent of readers preferring the human output . The Data2Story is the latest in a series of AI-powered newsrooms that work like a newsroom .

news.title: NYU finance professor Damodaran warns an AI crash
news.description: NYU finance professor Aswath Damodaran believes a potential AI crash would be more painful than the bursting of the dot-com bubble . The industry is building massive amounts of debt-financed physical infrastructure rather than lightweight software . The actual business model is to replace entire jobs, with unclear consequences for society, he says . Even if AI succeeds, he sees a problem with replacing entire jobs with unclear implications for society .

news.title: OpenAI's Codex can now watch you work once
news.description: OpenAI has released the "Record & Replay" feature for its Codex app on macOS . Users demonstrate a workflow once, Codex converts it into a reusable "skill," and then repeats it on its own . The feature isn't available in the EU, the UK, or Switzerland, or the U.S. It's not available yet in the UK or Switzerland . The Codex app can now watch you work once and repeat the task forever .

news.title: New benchmark exposes how badly AI struggles
news.description: Even the best AI model fails at realistic knowledge work, fully solving just 3 percent of tasks . Claude Fable 5, hits the highest pass rate but still nails all criteria on just 3% of tasks. Per-task costs span more than 800x, from about $0.04 for DeepSeek V4 Flash to over $31 . Weaker models choke on basic execution as they miss relevant files or spit out unusable results. Stronger models fail more quietly, as they hit the obvious requirements but miss details .

news.title: More people get news from AI chatbots, but
news.description: The Reuters Institute's Digital News Report 2026 finds that weekly use of AI chatbots for news has climbed from 7 to 10 percent globally . The growth is mostly coming from markets in Asia, Africa, Latin America, and Southern and Eastern Europe . Chatbot news use skews young and engaged, according to the study . The study's recommendation for publishers: don't try to compete with AI platforms on their own turf .

news.title: Amazon drops its OpenAI drama film after signing
news.description: Amazon MGM Studios has dropped "Artificial," the nearly finished OpenAI film directed by Luca Guadagnino with Andrew Garfield as Sam Altman . Amazon struck a $50 billion partnership with OpenAI in February . According to an insider, both Altman and Musk come off poorly in the film . The case raises questions about how business ties in the tech industry can limit creative freedom in tech industry . Amazon MGM Studio dropped its OpenAI drama film after signing a deal with Altman's company .

news.title: Google Deepmind loses another top AI researcher
news.description: Nobel Prize winner John Jumper is leaving Google Deepmind for Anthropic . Days earlier, Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer left for OpenAI . AlphaGo researcher David Silver started his own company . Weeks before that, AlphaGo player David Silver left Google to start his own AI firm AlphaGo . Three of Google's most prominent AI minds, gone within months, all of them within Google's Deepmind .

news.title: Norway bans generative AI tools in elementary schools
news.description: Norway is banning generative AI tools in elementary schools starting in late August . Students in grades 1 through 7 won't be allowed to use AI at all . Secondary schools will permit it only under supervision . Prime Minister Stoere says children must first "learn to read, write, and do math" He says kids must also learn to read and write to protect basic learning skills in order to be able to do math .

news.title: E2e-assure introduces Cumulo, the U.K.’
news.description: e2e-assure introduces Cumulo, the U.K.’s only sovereign, AI-driven, zero-day SOC platform to secure IT and OT environments . Built around digital twin technology and customer-dedicated AI models, Cumulo answers the recent announcement by GCHQ for AI Cyber Shield, enabling early identification of threats and vulnerabilities before incidents occur . Cumulo is designed to help organisations defend their IT/OT environments .

news.title: SAP and Google Cloud deploy agentic commerce architecture
news.description: SAP and Google Cloud are deploying agentic commerce architecture to automate multi-agent marketing and retail operations at enterprise scale . SAP research indicates 78 percent of businesses consider AI essential for retaining customers in 2026 . However, the same data reveals fewer than two in five companies share customer data across customer experience (37%) or CRM (39%) SAP research shows fewer than 2 in 5 companies share data across CRM or customer experience .

news.title: Website "In the Weights" shows whether AI models
news.description: Two former OpenAI employees have built a website called "In the Weights" that reveals which people AI models can recall purely from their training data . Mozart, Shakespeare, and Taylor Swift topped the list . A strength score of up to 996 shows how deeply a person is embedded in a person's memory . A score of 996 is a strength score, with Mozart and Shakespeare topping the list of people with a 996 score .

news.title: Google appeals ruling that made it directly liable
news.description: Germany's Munich Regional Court held the company directly liable for inaccurate AI search results . The AI had falsely linked two Munich-based publishers to fraud schemes . Google calls them "minor errors," but the court saw it differently . Google is appealing the ruling by Germany's regional court . The court saw them as 'minor mistakes' but Google called them "Minor errors" Google appeals ruling that made it directly liable .

news.title: OpenAI researchers show small doses of "beneficial trait"
news.description: OpenAI researchers show that reinforcement learning on desired behavioral traits like truthfulness and corrigibility works across domains . Training on health data also improved deception detection, and the model scored better on 44 out of 53 benchmarks . The approach differs from Anthropic's constitution-based method, which is similar to Anthropic’s Constitution-based approach, which uses constitution to train AI models on constitution and constitutionality, researchers say .

news.title: AI systems rival doctors in Nature studies, but
news.description: Two new studies published in Nature show that specialized AI systems diagnose diseases and make treatment decisions as well as physicians in simulated patient cases . Both systems run on base models that are already outdated . One result suggests the tech won't age well well, but one result suggests that tech won't age well, says The Decoder . The decryptionist says AI systems rival doctors in new Nature studies, but the tech isn't going to age well .

news.title: Google Deepmind treats its own AI agents like
news.description: Google Deepmind treats its own AI agents as potential insider threats . The company's new "AI Control Roadmap" ties security measures to measurable AI capabilities . Analysis of one million coding tasks shows most problems stem from overzealous agents, not malicious intent . Deepmind warns the window for global security standards is closing fast, warning that global standards are closing fast . It says most problems are caused by rogue AI agents with office keys .

news.title: Alleged China ties at SK Telecom alarmed US
news.description: Anthropic shut down its AI models Claude Mythos and Fable 5 . South Korean telecom giant SK Telecom had access to Mythos through Anthropic's partner program Project Glasswing . US officials grew alarmed over what they saw as SK Telecom's alleged ties to China . Amazon and five other companies reportedly triggered the government crackdown on Anthropic . The two incidents caused the White House to lose confidence in Anthropic and force both models offline .

news.title: Anthropic brings Artifacts to Claude Code, letting teams
news.description: Claude Code now supports artifacts, a feature already familiar from the Claude chat chat . It lets you turn results of a Claude Code session into interactive web pages and share them with your team . Anthropic says use cases range from PR walkthroughs and incident timelines to license audits and architecture overviews . The pages are generated from the full session context: code, connected tools, and chat history . When something changes, the page updates automatically at the same URL, complete with version history .

news.title: ChatGPT's new health upgrade beats doctor-written answers, OpenAI
news.description: OpenAI has upgraded ChatGPT's healthcare capabilities with the GPT-5.5 Instant model . The updated model matches the performance of the most expensive Thinking models on machine-based health tests like HealthBench and HealthBench Professional . The rate of incorrect health statements has dropped by 71 percent over the past two months, according to OpenAI . More than 230 million people use ChatGpt weekly for health-related questions, things like understanding lab results and prepping for doctor appointments .

news.title: Computer vision deployments drive retail productivity gains
news.description: Computer vision deployments are driving retail productivity gains as operators automate physical shelf tracking to protect eroding margins . This hardware deployment directly addresses the persistent in-store execution failures currently costing the industry billions . A study authored by Coresight Research – in partnership with technology providers Simbe and RELEX Solutions – calculates the exact cost of the deployment of the vision vision to retail stores . The study was published in conjunction with the release of AI News .

news.title: Google's Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer joins OpenAI after
news.description: Noam Shazeer is co-author of the landmark "Attention Is All You Need" paper and former co-lead of Google's Gemini models . He only returned to Google from Character.AI in 2024 as part of a $2.7 billion deal . After Karpathy's move to Anthropic, it's the second major AI hiring shake-up this year . It's a second major hiring shake up this year after Anthropic and Anthropic .

news.title: Adobe adds AI agents to Photoshop, Premiere
news.description: Adobe is rolling out its "creative agent" across its Creative Cloud apps and third-party AI platforms like ChatGPT and Claude . The agent orchestrates multi-step workflows while users just describe the end result . Adobe wants it to become the connecting layer between ideation, creation, and production . Each assistant is tuned to its specific app and users pick which tasks to hand off and which to do themselves . Adobe is also bolting new features onto the Firefly AI Assistant, already in public beta .

news.title: Midjourney, known for AI image generation, unveils
news.description: Midjourney is building a full-body ultrasound scanner and opening its own spa in San Francisco . The company says the technology should eventually surpass MRI without radiation or magnets . By 2031, the startup wants to deploy a fleet of more than 50,000 scanners capable of one billion scans per month . The first spa is supposed to open by the end of 2027, and a third-generation scanner is slated for 2028 .

news.title: Yann LeCun warns AI labs like OpenAI
news.description: Yann LeCun warns AI labs like OpenAI and Anthropic are heading for a "big bubble explosion" LeCUN's own startup, AMI Labs, raised $1 billion for an alternative approach to AI . His own AI lab, OpenAI, was founded by his own founder and CEO of the company AMI . He says operating costs aren't dropping fast enough and operations are effectively subsidized by investors, and operating costs are too high .

news.title: Microsoft sells OpenAI models in China but Anthropic
news.description: Microsoft has quietly become the main supplier of OpenAI models in China, selling the technology to the country's largest internet companies . OpenAI and Anthropic keep their own models out of the market on intellectual-property and misuse grounds . The arrangement hands Microsoft a position no other American AI vendor holds: The arrangement, detailed this week by Bloomberg, was reported by …Bloomberg . Microsoft: "No other American firm has a position like this in China"

news.title: HSBC expands AI banking partnership with Google Cloud
news.description: HSBC has entered a multi-year partnership with Google Cloud to develop and deploy artificial intelligence tools across its global operations . Announced at Google Cloud Summit London 2026, agreement covers work in wealth management, financial crime risk management, and internal decision support . HSBC will work with the Google Cloud and Google DeepMind engineering teams on AI . The partnership will also focus on developing and deploying AI tools across the company's global operations.

news.title: Zhipu AI's GLM-5.2 closes in on closed-source
news.description: Chinese AI lab Zhipu AI releases GLM-5.2 with stable 1-million-token context under MIT license . On FrontierSWE, a benchmark for hours-long coding tasks, the open-source model trails Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.8 by just one percentage point . On reasoning, it still falls well behind closed-source rivals in coding marathons, but still falls behind them . This summary highlights key aspects

news.title: Amazon, Nvidia, and AMD bet $310 million
news.description: Amazon, Nvidia, and AMD are putting $310 million into world model startup Odyssey ML . CIA-linked fund IQT and Google chief scientist Jeff Dean are also backing the round . Odyssey ML is now valued at $1.45 billion . World models are shaping up to be the next big AI bet after pure language models . The company is building 3D world models and is building its first AI world model system .

news.title: Microsoft researcher builds a working neural network out
news.description: A Microsoft researcher built a working neural network out of goats, bridges, and ice ramps in the Age of Empires II map editor . What looks like a joke is a pointed critique of AI research methods . His analysis of 315 papers found that more than half already assume language models have human-like traits before the experiment even starts . Replace the chat interface with wandering goats, and the math doesn't change, but the feeling that you're talking to someone does .

news.title: Berlin court rules Google's AI Overviews are just
news.description: Berlin court rules Google's AI-generated summaries are just a "new search result format" A perfume company had sued because AI search displayed its brand names alongside cheaper knockoffs and linked to their websites . The ruling partly contradicts a recent Munich decision that held Google directly liable for false AI responses, though the two cases are quite different . Google has no "decisive influence" over the content, the court rules .

news.title: Microsoft's Copilot Cowork moves to usage-based billing
news.description: Microsoft is weighing a fine-tuned version of Deepseek V4 as a cheaper model option for Copilot Cowork . The company is also switching to usage-based billing, since Copilot head Charles Lamanna says flat-rate pricing isn't sustainable . The move fits a broader pattern in the industry, with Microsoft weighing a similar move . Microsoft may tap DeepSeek V4 for a cheaper version of the service .

news.title: Hyperscalers may soon be unable to fund their
news.description: Microsoft, Amazon, Alphabet, Meta, and Oracle are growing their AI infrastructure spending by about 70 percent a year . While operating cash flow is only rising at 23 percent . If the trend holds, spending could overtake cash flow as early as Q3 2026 . Several of these companies are already tapping outside funding, Epoch AI analysis says . Hyperscalers may soon be unable to fund their AI buildout from cash flow alone .

news.title: OpenAI researchers want to predict how often
news.description: OpenAI researchers propose a way to predict how often a new AI model will make mistakes after release . The new approach uses real conversations that users had with a previous model . It could fill gaps left by standard safety testing, which is based on what real users actually type . The approach was tested on four models using roughly 1.3 million conversations from August 2025 through March 2026 . The simulation correctly predicted whether a problem would increase or decrease 92 percent of the time .

news.title: Nvidia research shows robots that train themselves through
news.description: Researchers from Nvidia, Carnegie Mellon University, and UC Berkeley are using AI coding agents to teach robots dexterous grasping in the real world . A fleet of eight robots hit up to 99 percent success on demanding tasks like sorting pins into a box and cutting a cable tie with a cutter . A breakthrough discovered at one station spreads across the entire fleet . On the Push-T test, going from one to eight agents cut the time to full success from about five hours to two .

news.title: Google Cloud generative AI automates council planning operations
news.description: Government ministries are deploying Google Cloud generative AI across municipal agencies to automate council planning operations . Public sector administration handles vast volumes of unstructured data that delay infrastructure development . The UK central government established a target to construct 1.5 million new homes by 2029 . Local planning authorities encounter administrative backlogs caused by dense paperwork, delaying infrastructure development, delaying development of new homes and infrastructure . The post: Google Cloud Generative AI automates council planning .

news.title: DeepSeek takes outside money for the first time
news.description: Chinese AI startup DeepSeek has raised more than 50 billion yuan - about $7.4 billion . The company's valuation now tops $50 billion . Tencent and battery maker CATL are among the biggest outside backers . Investors had to put their money into a limited partnership managed by CEO Liang Wenfeng . The deal structure is unusual, investors have no voting rights and face a five-year lock-up period .

news.title: Anthropic backs off unpopular billing overhaul as price
news.description: Anthropic planned to separate Agent SDK and third-party app usage from subscription limits . The original plan would have stopped the Agent SDK, the claude -p command, and apps from drawing on regular subscription limits starting June 15 . Anthropic has paused the billing overhaul it had announced for June 15, 2026 . For now, "Nothing changes for now," Anthropic says . The company says it's working to better align the plan with actual usage patterns .

news.title: How easily can Russian propaganda fool AI models?
news.description: The Institute of the Estonian Language has released a benchmark measuring how susceptible AI language models are to Russian propaganda . Sixty models were tested with 75 questions in three languages covering 14 propaganda narratives . Anthropic's Claude models claimed the top spots, followed by Nvidia's Nemotron 3 and Alibaba's Qwen 3.6 Plus . Mistral's models landed in the bottom third . The results line up with a Newsguard study that found Mistral had a steady misinformation rate of 36.67 percent .

news.title: SpaceX bets $60 billion on Cursor to catch
news.description: Elon Musk's SpaceX is buying AI coding startup Anysphere for $60 billion . The deal is meant to help the struggling xAI division catch up with Anthropic and OpenAI . Cursor is one of the fastest-growing software startups, with more than 3,000 customers paying at least $100,000 a year for the software . SpaceX confirmed on X that it's training a new AI model with Cursor .

news.title: How AI-Powered CMS Platforms are Transforming Enterprise Content
news.description: How AI-Powered CMS Platforms are Transforming Enterprise Content Operations? How do you get the right content, in the right format, to the right channel? The answer was usually a combination of manual processes, siloed systems, and large coordination teams that grew historically . The post: How AI powered CMS platforms are transforming enterprise content operations? Read more from the AI News blog here: http://://://www.AINews.com/AINews/ .

news.title: AI Red Teaming Explained: What it
news.description: With AI adoption accelerating, testing systems under adversarial conditions has become increasingly important . It enables organisations to identify vulnerabilities before deployment and strengthen overall system safety . Explore what AI red teaming is, why it matters and the leading companies offering AI red . teaming tests artificial artificial artificial systems under . adversarial . conditions and why it is important to test systems under the conditions of an AI system under adversary conditions . It is a tool that can be used to test and test systems before deployment .

news.title: EU publishes its AI content labelling playbook ahead
news.description: EU publishes its AI content labelling playbook ahead of the AI Act's August deadline . The voluntary Code of Practice is meant to help companies meet transparency rules that become law across the bloc on August 2 onwards . The European Commission released the final Code on 10 June, setting out practical steps for businesses that build and use generative AI to mark their content . The EU has now published the final code of Practice, which is voluntary .

news.title: Insurers pivot AI strategy toward core risk underwriting
news.description: Insurers are embedding AI technologies into workflows that directly influence underwriting discipline and capital allocation . AI investments by insurers are now expected to generate tangible business value beyond mere efficiency . Insurers pivot AI strategy toward core risk underwriting, says Christian Preece, Insurance Director at Evident, in the 2026 Evident AI Index . He says: “For years, insurers have competed on AI ambition,”

news.title: OpenAI burned through $34 billion last year
news.description: OpenAI spent $34 billion in the past year, according to independent journalist Ed Zitron . About $19 billion went to research and development, while $6 billion was spent on sales and marketing . By year's end, monthly revenue hit $2 billion, up from $1 billion per quarter at the end of 2024 . OpenAI is preparing an IPO that could value the company at more than $1 trillion .

news.title: Pokémon Go data helped train AI now linked
news.description: Developer Niantic added in-game incentives for Pokémon Go players to scan real-world locations with their smartphones . Millions of users then scanned streets, buildings, parks, and trees, generating billions of visual mapping data points . That technology is now being combined with a US defense contractor's software for GPS-free navigation . The system is immune to GPS jamming and spoofing, which is already being used in the wars in Ukraine and Iran to throw off kamikaze drones .

news.title: Nvidia joins AI debt boom with $20 billion
news.description: Nvidia wants to raise at least $20 billion through its first bond sale since 2021 . The chipmaker is offering bonds in seven tranches with maturities ranging from two to 30 years . The longest tranche carries a spread of about 0.9 percentage points above U.S. Treasuries . JPMorgan Chase, Morgan Stanley, and Goldman Sachs are among the banks managing the sale . Nvidia's last bond sale was in June 2021, when it raised $5 billion .

news.title: Anthropic may be asking Anthropic the impossible
news.description: Government officials appear to be accusing Anthropic of disregarding Trump's cyber directive and releasing Fable 5 without explicit approval . Anthropic's CEO Dario Amodei said back in 2023 that "a jailbreak could be life or death" Security experts and tech industry executives have published an open letter to Trade Secretary Lutnick and National Cyber Director Cairncross calling for export controls on Fable and Mythos to be lifted .

news.title: The AI Hype Index: AI gets booed
news.description: The AI Hype Index: AI gets booed in graduation season . Reese Witherspoon has warned women to embrace it or be replaced by it . The long-awaited V4 is more efficient and a win for Chinese chipmakers. The new T-Mobile phone plan takes a nuclear approach to online safety. We’re having trouble saving your preferences. Try refreshing this page and updating them one more time. If you continue to get this message, reach out at customer-service@technologyreview.com with a list of newsletters you’d like to receive.

news.title: How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template
news.description: Pope Leo XIV’s new encyclical on artificial intelligence includes a statement that warrants serious attention from technologists and policymakers: “Technology is never neutral” Magnifica Humanitas (“Magnificent Humanity”) is a clarion call to all people to act with courage and solidarity . Despite a lack of regulation, we still have the ability to steer artificial intelligence in ways that can benefit our common humanity .

news.title: Anthropic's export controls sparked a global AI sovereignty
news.description: Anthropic export controls turned an abstract policy fear into a live one last week . US government directive took the company's two most powerful AI models offline as of June 13, 2026 . Anthropic's own foreign-born employees were briefly taken offline for users everywhere, including, briefly, Anthropic . The move set off alarm bells across Europe and Canada about who really controls the AI industry . The AI off switch sparked a global AI sovereignty scramble .

news.title: Accenture: Consumers show growing trust in AI shopping
news.description: Consumers are showing a willingness to let AI agents take on more shopping-related tasks . 74% of respondents would trust a personal AI agent more than their best friend to make a shopping move . The company’s 2026 Consumer Pulse Research, based on a survey of 25,590 consumers across 16 countries, found that 74% would trust AI agents more than best friends to make decisions . The survey was based on an online survey conducted by Accenture in 16 countries .

news.title: HarmonyOS 7 steps into the AI gap Apple
news.description: The gap Apple could not fill, Huawei has moved into with an architecture built specifically for it . HarmonyOS 7 steps into the AI gap Apple left open in China . Apple confirmed that Siri AI would not launch in China, but Huawei has stepped in to fill the gap . The headline change is a change in the headline change, but it is not a major change in how the operating system has been designed . It is the beginning of the agent era, and the end of the Siri AI era .

news.title: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella warns of "a small
news.description: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella warns of a small number of AI systems capturing all the economic returns . Without it, he warns, a few large models could absorb the value of entire industries . The argument also happens to line up with the business logic of his own Azure platform, Microsoft's own AI capabilities built on internal data and proprietary learning loops . Microsoft CEO wants companies to build "token capital" alongside human capital .

news.title: Anthropic shutdown sparks sovereignty debate across Europe across
news.description: Anthropic shut down Fable 5 and Mythos 5 worldwide . European researchers debating the right response: building their own foundation models or securing access through contracts . But building out homegrown infrastructure would require computing capacity, energy, and competitive providers that Europe currently lacks, experts warn . The European Commission is assessing the implications of the US order that forced Anthropic to shut down the two games . The shutdown sparks sovereignty debate across Europe .

news.title: McDonald’s tests Google-backed AI drive-thru ordering system
news.description: McDonald’s is testing a new AI system that can take drive-thru orders and support restaurant operations . The system, called ArchIQ and nicknamed ‘Archy,” was introduced during the company’�s Worldwide convention . It is being tested at five McDonald's locations in the United States, though the company has not named the restaurants involved in testing the system . The company hasn't released the names of those restaurants involved .

news.title: Siri AI arrives with Google inside, and much
news.description: Apple's Stacey Ford, vice president of OS Program Management, was talking about Spotlight at WWDC 2026 . "We've all had that moment where you search for something you know is there, but it just won't show up," she said . Siri AI arrives with Google inside, and much of the world is locked out . Apple's Spotlight will launch on Monday at Apple Park, the thing that wouldn't happen .

news.title: Xebia: Why AI agents fail without the right
news.description: Agentic AI scales on data strength, as Niels Zeilemaker, global CTO at Xebia, explains . “If you don’t think about that, you can [AI agents] fail without the right data foundation,” he says . ‘If you’re adding AI agents to accelerate its processes, you have to start at the foundation – and that means making your data available for AI consumption .

news.title: Visa ChatGPT integration enables AI agent retail purchasing
news.description: Visa has linked its payment infrastructure to ChatGPT, enabling AI agents to recommend retail products and execute financial transactions . The deployment removes human intervention from the final stages of the retail funnel . Autonomous agents will now process user prompts, evaluate merchant catalogues, and complete the checkout process using Visa’s payment rails at any supporting merchant . Visa's payment rails can be used by any supporting merchants at any of them .

news.title: Coinbase for Agents: Automating portfolio trading
news.description: Coinbase for Agents connects AI to financial execution channels to automate trading and payments directly from user portfolios . Large language models process vast quantities of data but lack direct integration with active financial portfolios . Individuals frequently employ these models to evaluate market developments or research investment opportunities . These software tools possess the capacity for complex software tools that can be complex and difficult to work with complex software . The post: Automating portfolio trading with AI appeared in AI News.

news.title: C3 AI agents will automate predictive maintenance
news.description: Shell will use agents from C3 AI to shift from anomaly detection to fully-automated predictive maintenance . The global energy giant is building on their current use of the C3 . Reliability Suite already keeps tabs on more than 30,000 crucial pieces of equipment across upstream and downstream operations . Shell now intends to lean on C3 to lean towards automated maintenance, says C3. AI News . The post: How C3 agents will automate predictive maintenance for Shell .

news.title: Weis Markets adds Instacart AI-powered shopping carts
news.description: Weis Markets is adding Instacart’s AI-powered shopping carts, Caper Carts, to select stores in Pennsylvania . Smart carts bring digital coupons, loyalty features, and repeat-purchase recommendations into the grocery aisle . The smart carts include cameras, certified scales, location systems, and a touchscreen . Weis markets will deploy the smart carts, which include cameras and certified scales and location systems .

news.title: Aviva deploys AI to stop £230M in sophisticated
news.description: Aviva has uncovered a record £230 million in insurance fraud claims and is using AI tools to counter the growing problem . The battleground has changed, and culprits are also coming armed with a new generation of tools . We're now in an environment where AI is being used not just to defend against fraud, but also defend against the fraud, says AI News editor-at-a-professionals . Aviva deploys AI to stop £230M in sophisticated insurance fraud, he says .

news.title: Autonomous AI Data Loss in DevOps: Building Efficient
news.description: Autonomous AI agents are altering the speed at which software is shipped . Unfortunately, they are also shrinking the time it takes for a mistake to become a catastrophe . The threat no longer comes just from external ransomware or malicious insiders . It comes from authorized, internal tools, according to the author of the book . The book is published on Amazon.com/Artificial AI News. For more information on the book, visit Amazon.co.uk .

news.title: How to sign PDFs easily online
news.description: Signing PDFs has become an important task for businesses and individuals alike . The ability to quickly and securely sign PDFs online is essential . Fortunately, with the rise of online PDF signers, signing PDFs is now easier . Click here to learn more about how to sign your PDFs easily online with a PDF signer . Do you know how to do this? Share it with us on Facebook or contact us at iReport.

news.title: Claude Fable 5 outpaces GPT-5.5 by 13 points
news.description: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 hits 88 percent accuracy on the hardest FrontierMath tier . Opus 4.5 sat below 10 percent in early 2026 . OpenAI's GPT-5.5 reaches about 75 percent on the same tier . The pace of improvement in AI math keeps accelerating, Anthropic says . Anthropic: "Claude Fable" 5 outpaces GPT 5 by 13 points on FrontierMath's toughest problems .

news.title: Amazon and five other companies reportedly triggered
news.description: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy and executives from other tech companies reportedly warned the Trump administration about security vulnerabilities in Anthropic's Fable model . Within hours, the White House forced the model offline through an export control order . The move may well be a legitimate security policy decision, but it also reads as a show of force against an inconvenient company . Amazon and five other companies reportedly triggered the government crackdown on the Anthropic model .

news.title: AI coding agents find the right file but
news.description: AI coding agents like Claude Code or Codex reliably find the right file but miss most of the critical lines within it . The new SWE-Explore benchmark is the first to test code search separately from the actual repair . Without enough context, even the best fix will fail, and it shows that without enough context the best fixes will fail . The benchmark was the first time the benchmark has been tested separately from actual repairs .

news.title: KPMG fabricated AI case studies in a report
news.description: KPMG published a report on AI in business that contained fabricated case studies involving UBS, the NHS, and other organizations . GPTZero CEO Edward Tian, who helped uncover the errors, warns of "secondary hallucinations," flawed claims from trusted consulting firms that spread unchecked . The report was designed to sell clients on AI adoption, but has since pulled the report out of circulation . KPMg has since withdrawn the report .

news.title: Google Cloud's Open Knowledge Format turns scattered docs
news.description: Google Cloud's new Open Knowledge Format standardizes scattered organizational knowledge as Markdown files with YAML frontmatter . The minimalist spec formalizes a pattern Andrej Karpathy recently popularized as the "LLM Wiki" Google Cloud will use the format to make it portable and usable for AI agents . The new format formalizes the pattern, which has been popularized by Andrej Karpathy recently .

news.title: KPMG pulls report on AI usage due
news.description: Professional services firm KPMG pulls report on AI usage due to apparent hallucinations . GPTZero identified a number of inaccuracies in the report, which was published in October 2025 . UBS, the UK's National Health Service, Swiss Federal Railways, and Transport for London all told the FT the report’s claims about their AI usage were either untrue or misleading . EY withdrew a report on loyalty rewards programs that appeared to include fake footnotes and AI hallucinations .

news.title: Amazon CEO reportedly raised Anthropic model concerns before
news.description: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy reportedly raised concerns about Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models . Anthropic cut off worldwide access to two models on Friday . The government subsequently imposed an export control ban on the models . The Wall Street Journal reports that Jassy told Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and other government officials that Amazon researchers used the model to obtain information that could be used in cyberattacks . An Amazon spokesperson told the WSJ that the company does not “share the details of those discussions”

news.title: New AI model called "Count Anything" does exactly
news.description: "Count Anything" is intended to be the first AI model capable of counting objects in any type of image, from crowds to cell samples under a microscope . In a comparative test, it cuts the error rate in half compared to previous systems . However, the approach still struggles with extremely dense objects and ambiguous terms . The new AI model still struggles to count objects in very dense images and ambiguous words like "count anything"

news.title: OpenAI faces investigation from state attorneys general
news.description: A coalition of state attorneys general has reportedly opened an investigation into OpenAI . The company was served with a subpoena from New York’s attorney general on Friday . The subpoena sought documents related to a broad range of topics including advertising, user engagement and retention, model sycophancy, handling of consumer data and health data, and treatment of minors and seniors . A spokesperson told the WSJ that OpenAI is cooperating with the investigation .

news.title: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella admits he's a token-maxer
news.description: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella is warning against "token-maxing," throwing the most powerful AI models at every problem . Frontier models shouldn't be wasted on everyday tasks, he says . The marginal cost of productivity gains has to match the token cost . Still, he admits, "I'm like a token-maxer too. So it is addictive." Nadell admits he's a token maxer, too: " It &s addictive."

news.title: Google Research's Gemini-SQL2 tops text-to-SQL benchmarks
news.description: Google Research's Gemini-SQL2 turns natural language into executable queries . Built on Gemini 3.1 Pro, it tops the BIRD benchmark at 80.04 percent accuracy . Google says the technology could improve natural language features across its data services . Google Research: Technology could be used to improve data-driven services across the world . Back to Mail Online home . Back To the page you came from: http://www.mailonline.co.uk/newsquiz.com/storystory .

news.title: Microsoft's SkillOpt boosts GPT-5.5 by using nothing but
news.description: A simple Markdown file is enough to boost GPT-5.5 by about 23 points on procedural tasks . The same file transfers across models and agent environments like Codex and Claude Code . Microsoft and three Chinese universities have developed SkillOpt, a method that optimizes instruction documents for AI agents using principles from traditional model training . The method was developed by Microsoft, three universities and is available in the U.S.

news.title: Anthropic’s safety warnings may have just backfired —
news.description: Anthropic forced to shut off access to two of its most powerful AI models citing national security concerns . Anthropic says the underlying concern is a claimed jailbreak of Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 . The company says the government has provided only verbal evidence of a "potential narrow, non-universal jailbreak" Anthropic is widely expected to pursue an IPO this year and has staked much of its public identity on being the safety-conscious alternative to rivals .

news.title: Meta’s months-old AI unit is a soul-crushing gulag
news.description: A new report in Wired suggests the company's Applied AI team is on the verge of revolt . Employees describe being forced into the Applied AI group with no real choice: join or quit . Their assigned work? Generating puzzles and coding problems to train AI models . "It's literally the gulag," one employee told Wired. "Most people find the work soul-crushing," said another . CEO Mark Zuckerberg reportedly addressed the situation in an internal memo Friday .

news.title: Chinese cybercrime operation that used AI to scam
news.description: Chinese cybercrime operation that used AI to scam ‘hundreds of thousands of victims' sued by Google . Outsider Enterprise allegedly uses AI in its campaigns to send scam text messages impersonating Google and other brands . Group deployed 9,000 fake websites, one million fraudulent web domains, and 2.5 million texts sent to Android users in a two-week period . Google said it uses “AI-powered tools to fight AI-powered scams,” which enable the company to detect scams .

news.title: Jeff Bezos’s Prometheus raises $12B to build
news.description: Jeff Bezos's Prometheus raises $12B to build an ‘artificial general engineer' for the physical world . Bezos says productivity gains will lead to 'labor scarcity' in a world where demand for human workers outpaces supply . At $41 billion, Prometheus is one of the most richly valued AI startups ever funded . Bezos knows something about labor at scale: Amazon employs more than 1.5 million people worldwide .

news.title: SpaceX officially prices shares at $135
news.description: SpaceX officially prices shares at $135 in the largest IPO ever . Elon Musk's space and AI conglomerate officially confirmed that it has raised $75 billion from the sale of its shares to its underwriters . At this price, the deal looks set to make Musk the world's first trillionaire . The company, officially known as Space Exploration Technologies Corp., will trade under the SPCX ticker symbol on the Nasdaq stock exchange Friday .

news.title: Is It a Super El Niño Year? It
news.description: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration declared that the semiannual climate phenomenon has officially begun . El Niño is like the engine of a car: Fire it up and the atmosphere gets moving . NOAA gives this year’s El Niño a 63 percent chance of exceeding the 3.6-degree F threshold, which would qualify it as a ‘super El Niño’ Climate models are bullish that it could surpass that threshold by a wide margin .

news.title: Mexican World Cup Stadiums have FIFA’s Environmental Certifications
news.description: FIFA maintained the requirement that the venues must have LEED environmental certifications . FIFA also requires that they use natural grass, which is water-intensive to maintain . The BBVA stadium in Monterrey and the Akron stadium in Guadalajara, having been built in the last 15 years, were conceived under more modern parameters and already met some requirements . For a stadium like Azteca in Mexico City, which opened in 1966, this involved major transformations .

news.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS
news.description: Railway, a San Francisco-based cloud platform, has raised $100 million in a Series B funding round . The investment values Railway as one of the most significant infrastructure startups to emerge during the AI boom . The company claims its platform delivers deployments in under one second — fast enough to keep pace with AI-generated code . Customers report a tenfold increase in developer velocity and up to 65 percent cost savings compared to traditional cloud providers .

news.title: XAI fired engineer who raised alarms about Grok
news.description: xAI fired an engineer who raised alarms about Grok safety, new lawsuit claims . Devin Kim claims he was fired for raising concerns about AI safety . Kim was concerned about the possibility that Grok could foment discrimination and spread information about weapons of mass destruction . Kim's focus on AI safety predates his time at Scale AI, where he led a project that produced training data for AI to train systems to detect harmful content and comply with governance policies .

news.title: Amazon borrows $17.5B from banks as AI spending
news.description: Amazon has signed a deal to borrow some $17.5 billion from a number of financial lenders . The banks behind the loan reportedly include Citigroup, JPMorgan Chase, Wells Fargo, HSBC, and BofA Securities . The deal has been characterized as a delayed draw term loan, meaning Amazon can draw down the funds on its own timeline rather than taking the full sum upfront . The loan comes just two days after it was reported that Amazon would raise $14 billion in a Canadian bond sale .

news.title: China Opens World’s First Wind-Powered Underwater Data Center
news.description: China has become the first country in the world to operate an underwater data center powered by wind . With an initial capacity of 24 megawatts, the facility is submerged at a depth of 10 meters in the Lin-gang Special Zone, within the China Pilot Free Trade Zone in Shanghai . This location allows seawater to be used as a natural cooling system, reducing the proportion of energy used to cool the infrastructure to less than 10 percent .

news.title: There’s Finally Video of a Great White Shark
news.description: A diver recently documented his encounter with a great white shark in the Strait of Sicily . The footage shows a huge adult male specimen of Carcharodon carcharias, a native species that is now considered critically endangered . The species is increasingly endangered by human activities, particularly accidental capture in fishing operations, illegal fishing, habitat loss and the decline of its natural prey . Every documented sighting in the Mediterranean represents a valuable opportunity for scientific research and conservation strategies .

news.title: Google just redesigned the search box
news.description: Google announced a sweeping redesign of its search box at its annual I/O developer conference . The new search box expands, accepts files, and coaches you on what to ask . Google is merging AI overviews and AI Mode features into a single, seamless search flow . The changes show a fundamental shift in how Google expects people to interact with the product that generates the vast majority of Alphabet's revenue . It is the clearest signal yet that Google views the future of its flagship product as an interface where users hold open-ended, multimodal conversations with an AI system...
